sections.name: Policy and Procedures
sections.description:
a. Develop, document, and disseminate to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]:
  1\. [Selection (one or more): Organization-level; Mission/business process-level; System-level] access control policy that:
    (a) Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and
    (b) Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and
  2\. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and the associated access controls;

b. Designate an [Assignment: organization-defined official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the access control policy and procedures; and

c. Review and update the current access control:
  1\. Policy [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events]; and
  2\. Procedures [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events].

sections.name: Account Management
Account Management | Automated System Account Management
sections.description: Support the management of system accounts using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Account Management | Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
sections.description: Automatically [Selection: remove; disable] temporary and emergency accounts after [Assignment: organization-defined time period for each type of account].

sections.name: Account Management | Disable Accounts
Account Management | Automated Audit Actions
sections.description: Automatically audit account creation, modification, enabling, disabling, and removal actions.

sections.name: Account Management | Inactivity Logout
sections.description: Require that users log out when [Assignment: organization-defined time period of expected inactivity or description of when to log out].

sections.name: Account Management | Dynamic Privilege Management
sections.description: Implement [Assignment: organization-defined dynamic privilege management capabilities].

sections.name: Account Management | Privileged User Accounts
Account Management | Dynamic Account Management
sections.description: Create, activate, manage, and deactivate [Assignment: organization-defined system accounts] dynamically.

sections.name: Account Management | Restrictions on Use of Shared and Group Accounts
sections.description: Only permit the use of shared and group accounts that meet [Assignment: organization-defined conditions for establishing shared and group accounts].

sections.name: Account Management | Usage Conditions
sections.description: Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances and/or usage conditions] for  [Assignment: organization-defined system accounts].

sections.name: Account Management | Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
sections.description:
(a) Monitor system accounts for [Assignment: organization-defined atypical usage]; and

(b) Report atypical usage of system accounts to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles].

sections.name: Account Management | Disable Accounts for High-risk Individuals
sections.description: Disable accounts of individuals within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] of discovery of [Assignment: organization-defined significant risks].

sections.name: Access Enforcement
sections.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Dual Authorization
sections.description: Enforce dual authorization for [Assignment: organization-defined privileged commands and/or other organization-defined actions].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Mandatory Access Control
Access Enforcement | Discretionary Access Control
Access Enforcement | Security-relevant Information
sections.description: Prevent access to [Assignment: organization-defined security-relevant information] except during secure, non-operable system states.

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Role-based Access Control
sections.description: Enforce a role-based access control policy over defined subjects and objects and control access based upon [Assignment: organization-defined roles and users authorized to assume such roles].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Revocation of Access Authorizations
sections.description: Enforce the revocation of access authorizations resulting from changes to the security attributes of subjects and objects based on [Assignment: organization-defined rules governing the timing of revocations of access authorizations].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Controlled Release
sections.description:
Release information outside of the system only if:

(a) The receiving [Assignment: organization-defined system or system component] provides [Assignment: organization-defined controls]; and

(b) [Assignment: organization-defined controls] are used to validate the appropriateness of the information designated for release.

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Audited Override of Access Control Mechanisms
sections.description: Employ an audited override of automated access control mechanisms under [Assignment: organization-defined conditions] by [Assignment: organization-defined roles].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Restrict Access to Specific Information Types
sections.description: Restrict access to data repositories containing [Assignment: organization-defined information types].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Assert and Enforce Application Access
Access Enforcement | Attribute-based Access Control
sections.description: Enforce attribute-based access control policy over defined subjects and objects and control access based upon [Assignment: organization-defined attributes to assume access permissions].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Individual Access
sections.description: Provide [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms] to enable individuals to have access to the following elements of their personally identifiable information: [Assignment: organization-defined elements].

sections.name: Access Enforcement | Discretionary and Mandatory Access Control
sections.description:
(a) Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined mandatory access control policy] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy; and

(b) Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined discretionary access control policy] over the set of covered subjects and objects specified in the policy.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement
sections.description: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between connected systems based on [Assignment: organization-defined information flow control policies].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Object Security and Privacy Attributes
sections.description: Use [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy attributes] associated with [Assignment: organization-defined information, source, and destination objects] to enforce [Assignment: organization-defined information flow control policies] as a basis for flow control decisions.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Processing Domains
sections.description: Use protected processing domains to enforce [Assignment: organization-defined information flow control policies] as a basis for flow control decisions.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Dynamic Information Flow Control
sections.description: Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined information flow control policies].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Flow Control of Encrypted Information
sections.description: Prevent encrypted information from bypassing [Assignment: organization-defined information flow control mechanisms] by [Selection (one or more): decrypting the information; blocking the flow of the encrypted information; terminating communications sessions attempting to pass encrypted information; [Assignment: organization-defined procedure or method]].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Embedded Data Types
sections.description: Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined limitations] on embedding data types within other data types.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Metadata
sections.description: Enforce information flow control based on [Assignment: organization-defined metadata].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | One-way Flow Mechanisms
sections.description: Enforce one-way information flows through hardware-based flow control mechanisms.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Security and Privacy Policy Filters
sections.description:
(a) Enforce information flow control using [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy filters] as a basis for flow control decisions for [Assignment: organization-defined information flows]; and

(b) [Selection (one or more): Block; Strip; Modify; Quarantine] data after a filter processing failure in accordance with [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Human Reviews
sections.description: Enforce the use of human reviews for [Assignment: organization-defined information flows] under the following conditions: [Assignment: organization-defined conditions].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Enable and Disable Security or Privacy Policy Filters
sections.description: Provide the capability for privileged administrators to enable and disable [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy filters] under the following conditions: [Assignment: organization-defined conditions].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Configuration of Security or Privacy Policy Filters
sections.description: Provide the capability for privileged administrators to configure [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy filters] to support different security or privacy policies.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Data Type Identifiers
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, use [Assignment: organization-defined data type identifiers] to validate data essential for information flow decisions.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Decomposition into Policy-relevant Subcomponents
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, decompose information into [Assignment: organization-defined policy-relevant subcomponents] for submission to policy enforcement mechanisms.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Security or Privacy Policy Filter Constraints
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, implement [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy filters] requiring fully enumerated formats that restrict data structure and content.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Detection of Unsanctioned Information
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, examine the information for the presence of [Assignment: organization-defined unsanctioned information] and prohibit the transfer of such information in accordance with the [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Domain Authentication
sections.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate source and destination points by [Selection (one or more): organization; system; application; service; individual] for information transfer.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Validation of Metadata
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, implement [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy policy filters] on metadata.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Approved Solutions
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined solutions in approved configurations] to control the flow of [Assignment: organization-defined information] across security domains.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows
sections.description: Separate information flows logically or physically using [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms and/or techniques] to accomplish [Assignment: organization-defined required separations by types of information].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Access Only
sections.description: Provide access from a single device to computing platforms, applications, or data residing in multiple different security domains, while preventing information flow between the different security domains.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Modify Non-releasable Information
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, modify non-releasable information by implementing [Assignment: organization-defined modification action].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Internal Normalized Format
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, parse incoming data into an internal normalized format and regenerate the data to be consistent with its intended specification.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Data Sanitization
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, sanitize data to minimize [Selection (one or more): delivery of malicious content, command and control of malicious code, malicious code augmentation, and steganography encoded data; spillage of sensitive information] in accordance with [Assignment: organization-defined policy]].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Audit Filtering Actions
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, record and audit content filtering actions and results for the information being filtered.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Redundant/independent Filtering Mechanisms
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, implement content filtering solutions that provide redundant and independent filtering mechanisms for each data type.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Linear Filter Pipelines
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, implement a linear content filter pipeline that is enforced with discretionary and mandatory access controls.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Filter Orchestration Engines
sections.description:
When transferring information between different security domains, employ content filter orchestration engines to ensure that:

(a) Content filtering mechanisms successfully complete execution without errors; and

(b) Content filtering actions occur in the correct order and comply with [Assignment: organization-defined policy].

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Filter Mechanisms Using Multiple Processes
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, implement content filtering mechanisms using multiple processes.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Failed Content Transfer Prevention
sections.description: When transferring information between different security domains, prevent the transfer of failed content to the receiving domain.

sections.name: Information Flow Enforcement | Process Requirements for Information Transfer
Separation of Duties
sections.description:
a. Identify and document [Assignment: organization-defined duties of individuals requiring separation]; and

b. Define system access authorizations to support separation of duties.

sections.name: Least Privilege
sections.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) that are necessary to accomplish assigned organizational tasks.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Authorize Access to Security Functions
sections.description:
Authorize access for [Assignment: organization-defined individuals or roles] to:

(a) [Assignment: organization-defined security functions (deployed in hardware, software, and firmware)]; and

(b) [Assignment: organization-defined security-relevant information].

sections.name: Least Privilege | Non-privileged Access for Nonsecurity Functions
sections.description: Require that users of system accounts (or roles) with access to [Assignment: organization-defined security functions or security-relevant information] use non-privileged accounts or roles, when accessing nonsecurity functions.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Network Access to Privileged Commands
sections.description: Authorize network access to [Assignment: organization-defined privileged commands] only for [Assignment: organization-defined compelling operational needs] and document the rationale for such access in the security plan for the system.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Separate Processing Domains
sections.description: Provide separate processing domains to enable finer-grained allocation of user privileges.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Privileged Accounts
sections.description: Restrict privileged accounts on the system to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles].

sections.name: Least Privilege | Privileged Access by Non-organizational Users
sections.description: Prohibit privileged access to the system by non-organizational users.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Review of User Privileges
sections.description:
(a) Review [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] the privileges assigned to [Assignment: organization-defined roles or classes of users] to validate the need for such privileges; and

(b) Reassign or remove privileges, if necessary, to correctly reflect organizational mission and business needs.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Privilege Levels for Code Execution
sections.description: Prevent the following software from executing at higher privilege levels than users executing the software: [Assignment: organization-defined software].

sections.name: Least Privilege | Log Use of Privileged Functions
sections.description: Log the execution of privileged functions.

sections.name: Least Privilege | Prohibit Non-privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions
sections.description: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions.

sections.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
sections.description:
a. Enforce a limit of [Assignment: organization-defined number] consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a [Assignment: organization-defined time period]; and

b. Automatically [Selection (one or more): lock the account or node for an [Assignment: organization-defined time period]; lock the account or node until released by an administrator; delay next logon prompt per [Assignment: organization-defined delay algorithm]; notify system administrator; take other [Assignment: organization-defined action]] when the maximum number of unsuccessful attempts is exceeded.

sections.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts | Purge or Wipe Mobile Device
sections.description: Purge or wipe information from [Assignment: organization-defined mobile devices] based on [Assignment: organization-defined purging or wiping requirements and techniques] after [Assignment: organization-defined number] consecutive, unsuccessful device logon attempts.

sections.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts | Biometric Attempt Limiting
sections.description: Limit the number of unsuccessful biometric logon attempts to [Assignment: organization-defined number].

sections.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts | Use of Alternate Authentication Factor
sections.description:
(a) Allow the use of [Assignment: organization-defined authentication factors] that are different from the primary authentication factors after the number of organization-defined consecutive invalid logon attempts have been exceeded; and

(b) Enforce a limit of [Assignment: organization-defined number] consecutive invalid logon attempts through use of the alternative factors by a user during a [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

sections.name: System Use Notification
Previous Logon Notification
sections.description: Notify the user, upon successful logon to the system, of the date and time of the last logon.

sections.name: Previous Logon Notification | Unsuccessful Logons
sections.description: Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the number of unsuccessful logon attempts since the last successful logon.

sections.name: Previous Logon Notification | Successful and Unsuccessful Logons
sections.description: Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the number of [Selection: successful logons; unsuccessful logon attempts; both] during [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

sections.name: Previous Logon Notification | Notification of Account Changes
sections.description: Notify the user, upon successful logon, of changes to [Assignment: organization-defined security-related characteristics or parameters of the user’s account] during [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

sections.name: Previous Logon Notification | Additional Logon Information
sections.description: Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the following additional information: [Assignment: organization-defined additional information].

sections.name: Concurrent Session Control
sections.description: Limit the number of concurrent sessions for each [Assignment: organization-defined account and/or account type] to [Assignment: organization-defined number].

sections.name: Device Lock
sections.description:
a. Prevent further access to the system by [Selection (one or more): initiating a device lock after [Assignment: organization-defined time period] of inactivity; requiring the user to initiate a device lock before leaving the system unattended]; and

b. Retain the device lock until the user reestablishes access using established identification and authentication procedures.

sections.name: Device Lock | Pattern-hiding Displays
sections.description: Conceal, via the device lock, information previously visible on the display with a publicly viewable image.

sections.name: Session Termination
sections.description: Automatically terminate a user session after [Assignment: organization-defined conditions or trigger events requiring session disconnect].

sections.name: Session Termination | User-initiated Logouts
sections.description: Provide a logout capability for user-initiated communications sessions whenever authentication is used to gain access to [Assignment: organization-defined information resources].

sections.name: Session Termination | Termination Message
sections.description: Display an explicit logout message to users indicating the termination of authenticated communications sessions.

sections.name: Session Termination | Timeout Warning Message
sections.description: Display an explicit message to users indicating that the session will end in [Assignment: organization-defined time until end of session].

sections.name: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
sections.description:
a. Identify [Assignment: organization-defined user actions] that can be performed on the system without identification or authentication consistent with organizational mission and business functions; and

b. Document and provide supporting rationale in the security plan for the system, user actions not requiring identification or authentication.

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes
Security and Privacy Attributes | Dynamic Attribute Association
sections.description: Dynamically associate security and privacy attributes with [Assignment: organization-defined subjects and objects] in accordance with the following security and privacy policies as information is created and combined: [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy policies].

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Attribute Value Changes by Authorized Individuals
sections.description: Provide authorized individuals (or processes acting on behalf of individuals) the capability to define or change the value of associated security and privacy attributes.

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Maintenance of Attribute Associations by System
sections.description: Maintain the association and integrity of [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy attributes] to [Assignment: organization-defined subjects and objects].

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Association of Attributes by Authorized Individuals
sections.description: Provide the capability to associate [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy attributes] with [Assignment: organization-defined subjects and objects] by authorized individuals (or processes acting on behalf of individuals).

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Attribute Displays on Objects to Be Output
sections.description: Display security and privacy attributes in human-readable form on each object that the system transmits to output devices to identify [Assignment: organization-defined special dissemination, handling, or distribution instructions] using [Assignment: organization-defined human-readable, standard naming conventions].

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Maintenance of Attribute Association
sections.description: Require personnel to associate and maintain the association of [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy attributes] with [Assignment: organization-defined subjects and objects] in accordance with [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy policies].

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Consistent Attribute Interpretation
sections.description: Provide a consistent interpretation of security and privacy attributes transmitted between distributed system components.

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Association Techniques and Technologies
sections.description: Implement [Assignment: organization-defined techniques and technologies] in associating security and privacy attributes to information.

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Attribute Reassignment — Regrading Mechanisms
sections.description: Change security and privacy attributes associated with information only via regrading mechanisms validated using [Assignment: organization-defined techniques or procedures].

sections.name: Security and Privacy Attributes | Attribute Configuration by Authorized Individuals
sections.description: Provide authorized individuals the capability to define or change the type and value of security and privacy attributes available for association with subjects and objects.

sections.name: Remote Access
sections.description:
a. Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed; and

b. Authorize each type of remote access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

sections.name: Remote Access | Monitoring and Control
sections.description: Employ automated mechanisms to monitor and control remote access methods.

sections.name: Remote Access | Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
sections.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions.

sections.name: Remote Access | Managed Access Control Points
sections.description: Route remote accesses through authorized and managed network access control points.

sections.name: Remote Access | Privileged Commands and Access
sections.description:
(a) Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [Assignment: organization-defined needs]; and

(b) Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

sections.name: Remote Access | Protection of Mechanism Information
sections.description: Protect information about remote access mechanisms from unauthorized use and disclosure.

sections.name: Remote Access | Disconnect or Disable Access
sections.description: Provide the capability to disconnect or disable remote access to the system within [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

sections.name: Remote Access | Authenticate Remote Commands
sections.description: Implement [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms] to authenticate [Assignment: organization-defined remote commands].

sections.name: Wireless Access
sections.description:
a. Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of wireless access; and

b. Authorize each type of wireless access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

sections.name: Wireless Access | Authentication and Encryption
sections.description: Protect wireless access to the system using authentication of [Selection (one or more): users; devices] and encryption.

sections.name: Wireless Access | Disable Wireless Networking
sections.description: Disable, when not intended for use, wireless networking capabilities embedded within system components prior to issuance and deployment.

sections.name: Wireless Access | Restrict Configurations by Users
sections.description: Identify and explicitly authorize users allowed to independently configure wireless networking capabilities.

sections.name: Wireless Access | Antennas and Transmission Power Levels
sections.description: Select radio antennas and calibrate transmission power levels to reduce the probability that signals from wireless access points can be received outside of organization-controlled boundaries.

sections.name: Access Control for Mobile Devices
sections.description:
a. Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices, to include when such devices are outside of controlled areas; and

b. Authorize the connection of mobile devices to organizational systems.

sections.name: Access Control for Mobile Devices | Restrictions for Classified Information
sections.description:
(a) Prohibit the use of unclassified mobile devices in facilities containing systems processing, storing, or transmitting classified information unless specifically permitted by the authorizing official; and

(b) Enforce the following restrictions on individuals permitted by the authorizing official to use unclassified mobile devices in facilities containing systems processing, storing, or transmitting classified information:
  (1) Connection of unclassified mobile devices to classified systems is prohibited;
  (2) Connection of unclassified mobile devices to unclassified systems requires approval from the authorizing official;
  (3) Use of internal or external modems or wireless interfaces within the unclassified mobile devices is prohibited; and
  (4) Unclassified mobile devices and the information stored on those devices are subject to random reviews and inspections by [Assignment: organization-defined security officials], and if classified information is found, the incident handling policy is followed.

(c) Restrict the connection of classified mobile devices to classified systems in accordance with [Assignment: organization-defined security policies].

sections.name: Access Control for Mobile Devices | Full Device or Container-based Encryption
sections.description: Employ [Selection: full-device encryption; container-based encryption] to protect the confidentiality and integrity of information on [Assignment: organization-defined mobile devices].

sections.name: Use of External Systems
sections.description:
a. [Selection (one or more): Establish [Assignment: organization-defined terms and conditions]; Identify [Assignment: organization-defined controls asserted to be implemented on external systems]], consistent with the trust relationships established with other organizations owning, operating, and/or maintaining external systems, allowing authorized individuals to:
  1\. Access the system from external systems; and
  2\. Process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information using external systems; or

b. Prohibit the use of [Assignment: organizationally-defined types of external systems].

sections.name: Use of External Systems | Limits on Authorized Use
sections.description:
Permit authorized individuals to use an external system to access the system or to process, store, or transmit organization-controlled information only after:

(a) Verification of the implementation of controls on the external system as specified in the organization’s security and privacy policies and security and privacy plans; or

(b) Retention of approved system connection or processing agreements with the organizational entity hosting the external system.

sections.name: Use of External Systems | Portable Storage Devices — Restricted Use
sections.description: Restrict the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems using [Assignment: organization-defined restrictions].

sections.name: Use of External Systems | Non-organizationally Owned Systems — Restricted Use
sections.description: Restrict the use of non-organizationally owned systems or system components to process, store, or transmit organizational information using [Assignment: organization-defined restrictions].

sections.name: Use of External Systems | Network Accessible Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
sections.description: Prohibit the use of [Assignment: organization-defined network accessible storage devices] in external systems.

sections.name: Use of External Systems | Portable Storage Devices — Prohibited Use
sections.description: Prohibit the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems.

sections.name: Information Sharing
sections.description:
a. Enable authorized users to determine whether access authorizations assigned to a sharing partner match the information’s access and use restrictions for [Assignment: organization-defined information sharing circumstances where user discretion is required]; and

b. Employ [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms or manual processes] to assist users in making information sharing and collaboration decisions.

sections.name: Information Sharing | Automated Decision Support
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms] to enforce information-sharing decisions by authorized users based on access authorizations of sharing partners and access restrictions on information to be shared.

sections.name: Information Sharing | Information Search and Retrieval
sections.description: Implement information search and retrieval services that enforce [Assignment: organization-defined information sharing restrictions].

sections.name: Publicly Accessible Content
Data Mining Protection
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined data mining prevention and detection techniques] for [Assignment: organization-defined data storage objects] to detect and protect against unauthorized data mining.

sections.name: Access Control Decisions
sections.description: [Selection: Establish procedures; Implement mechanisms] to ensure [Assignment: organization-defined access control decisions] are applied to each access request prior to access enforcement.

sections.name: Access Control Decisions | Transmit Access Authorization Information
sections.description: Transmit [Assignment: organization-defined access authorization information] using [Assignment: organization-defined controls] to [Assignment: organization-defined systems] that enforce access control decisions.

sections.name: Access Control Decisions | No User or Process Identity
sections.description: Enforce access control decisions based on [Assignment: organization-defined security or privacy attributes] that do not include the identity of the user or process acting on behalf of the user.

sections.name: Reference Monitor
sections.description: Implement a reference monitor for [Assignment: organization-defined access control policies] that is tamperproof, always invoked, and small enough to be subject to analysis and testing, the completeness of which can be assured.

sections.name: Policy and Procedures
sections.description:
a. Develop, document, and disseminate to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]:
  1\. [Selection (one or more): Organization-level; Mission/business process-level; System-level] awareness and training policy that:
    (a) Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance; and
    (b) Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines; and
  2\. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the awareness and training policy and the associated awareness and training controls;

b. Designate an [Assignment: organization-defined official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the awareness and training policy and procedures; and

c. Review and update the current awareness and training:
  1\. Policy [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events]; and
  2\. Procedures [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events].

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness
Literacy Training and Awareness | Practical Exercises
sections.description: Provide practical exercises in literacy training that simulate events and incidents.

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness | Insider Threat
sections.description: Provide literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness | Social Engineering and Mining
sections.description: Provide literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential and actual instances of social engineering and social mining.

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness | Suspicious Communications and Anomalous System Behavior
sections.description: Provide literacy training on recognizing suspicious communications and anomalous behavior in organizational systems using [Assignment: organization-defined indicators of malicious code].

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness | Advanced Persistent Threat
sections.description: Provide literacy training on the advanced persistent threat.

sections.name: Literacy Training and Awareness | Cyber Threat Environment
sections.description:
(a) Provide literacy training on the cyber threat environment; and

(b) Reflect current cyber threat information in system operations.

sections.name: Role-based Training
sections.description:
a. Provide role-based security and privacy training to personnel with the following roles and responsibilities: [Assignment: organization-defined roles and responsibilities]:
  1\. Before authorizing access to the system, information, or performing assigned duties, and [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] thereafter; and
  2\. When required by system changes;

b. Update role-based training content [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events]; and

c. Incorporate lessons learned from internal or external security incidents or breaches into role-based training.

sections.name: Role-based Training | Environmental Controls
sections.description: Provide [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] with initial and [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] training in the employment and operation of environmental controls.

sections.name: Role-based Training | Physical Security Controls
sections.description: Provide [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] with initial and [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] training in the employment and operation of physical security controls.

sections.name: Role-based Training | Practical Exercises
sections.description: Provide practical exercises in security and privacy training that reinforce training objectives.

sections.name: Role-based Training | Processing Personally Identifiable Information
sections.description: Provide [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] with initial and [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] training in the employment and operation of personally identifiable information processing and transparency controls.

sections.name: Training Records
sections.description:
a. Document and monitor information security and privacy training activities, including security and privacy awareness training and specific role-based security and privacy training; and

sections.name: Event Logging
Content of Audit Records
Content of Audit Records | Additional Audit Information
sections.description: Generate audit records containing the following additional information: [Assignment: organization-defined additional information].

sections.name: Content of Audit Records | Limit Personally Identifiable Information Elements
sections.description: Limit personally identifiable information contained in audit records to the following elements identified in the privacy risk assessment: [Assignment: organization-defined elements].

sections.name: Audit Log Storage Capacity
sections.description: Allocate audit log storage capacity to accommodate [Assignment: organization-defined audit log retention requirements].

sections.name: Audit Log Storage Capacity | Transfer to Alternate Storage
sections.description: Transfer audit logs [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] to a different system, system component, or media other than the system or system component conducting the logging.

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
sections.description:
a. Alert [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] in the event of an audit logging process failure; and

b. Take the following additional actions: [Assignment: organization-defined additional actions].

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Storage Capacity Warning
sections.description: Provide a warning to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel, roles, and/or locations] within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when allocated audit log storage volume reaches [Assignment: organization-defined percentage] of repository maximum audit log storage capacity.

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Real-time Alerts
sections.description: Provide an alert within [Assignment: organization-defined real-time period] to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel, roles, and/or locations] when the following audit failure events occur: [Assignment: organization-defined audit logging failure events requiring real-time alerts].

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Configurable Traffic Volume Thresholds
sections.description: Enforce configurable network communications traffic volume thresholds reflecting limits on audit log storage capacity and [Selection: reject; delay] network traffic above those thresholds.

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Shutdown on Failure
sections.description: Invoke a [Selection: full system shutdown; partial system shutdown; degraded operational mode with limited mission or business functionality available] in the event of [Assignment: organization-defined audit logging failures], unless an alternate audit logging capability exists.

sections.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Alternate Audit Logging Capability
sections.description: Provide an alternate audit logging capability in the event of a failure in primary audit logging capability that implements [Assignment: organization-defined alternate audit logging functionality].

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Automated Process Integration
sections.description: Integrate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Correlate Audit Record Repositories
sections.description: Analyze and correlate audit records across different repositories to gain organization-wide situational awareness.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Central Review and Analysis
sections.description: Provide and implement the capability to centrally review and analyze audit records from multiple components within the system.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
sections.description: Integrate analysis of audit records with analysis of [Selection (one or more): vulnerability scanning information; performance data; system monitoring information; [Assignment: organization-defined data/information collected from other sources]] to further enhance the ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Correlation with Physical Monitoring
sections.description: Correlate information from audit records with information obtained from monitoring physical access to further enhance the ability to identify suspicious, inappropriate, unusual, or malevolent activity.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Permitted Actions
sections.description: Specify the permitted actions for each [Selection (one or more): system process; role; user] associated with the review, analysis, and reporting of audit record information.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Full Text Analysis of Privileged Commands
sections.description: Perform a full text analysis of logged privileged commands in a physically distinct component or subsystem of the system, or other system that is dedicated to that analysis.

sections.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Correlation with Information from Nontechnical Sources
sections.description: Correlate information from nontechnical sources with audit record information to enhance organization-wide situational awareness.

sections.name: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
sections.description:
Provide and implement an audit record reduction and report generation capability that:

a. Supports on-demand audit record review, analysis, and reporting requirements and after-the-fact investigations of incidents; and

b. Does not alter the original content or time ordering of audit records.

sections.name: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation | Automatic Processing
sections.description: Provide and implement the capability to process, sort, and search audit records for events of interest based on the following content: [Assignment: organization-defined fields within audit records].

sections.name: Time Stamps
sections.description:
a. Use internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records; and

b. Record time stamps for audit records that meet [Assignment: organization-defined granularity of time measurement] and that use Coordinated Universal Time, have a fixed local time offset from Coordinated Universal Time, or that include the local time offset as part of the time stamp.

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information
sections.description:
a. Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion; and

b. Alert [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] upon detection of unauthorized access, modification, or deletion of audit information.

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Hardware Write-once Media
sections.description: Write audit trails to hardware-enforced, write-once media.

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
sections.description: Store audit records [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] in a repository that is part of a physically different system or system component than the system or component being audited.

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Cryptographic Protection
sections.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit information and audit tools.

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Access by Subset of Privileged Users
sections.description: Authorize access to management of audit logging functionality to only [Assignment: organization-defined subset of privileged users or roles].

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Dual Authorization
sections.description: Enforce dual authorization for [Selection (one or more): movement; deletion] of [Assignment: organization-defined audit information].

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Read-only Access
sections.description: Authorize read-only access to audit information to [Assignment: organization-defined subset of privileged users or roles].

sections.name: Protection of Audit Information | Store on Component with Different Operating System
sections.description: Store audit information on a component running a different operating system than the system or component being audited.

sections.name: Non-repudiation
sections.description: Provide irrefutable evidence that an individual (or process acting on behalf of an individual) has performed [Assignment: organization-defined actions to be covered by non-repudiation].

sections.name: Non-repudiation | Association of Identities
sections.description:
(a) Bind the identity of the information producer with the information to [Assignment: organization-defined strength of binding]; and

(b) Provide the means for authorized individuals to determine the identity of the producer of the information.

sections.name: Non-repudiation | Validate Binding of Information Producer Identity
sections.description:
(a) Validate the binding of the information producer identity to the information at [Assignment: organization-defined frequency]; and

(b) Perform [Assignment: organization-defined actions] in the event of a validation error.

sections.name: Non-repudiation | Chain of Custody
sections.description: Maintain reviewer or releaser credentials within the established chain of custody for information reviewed or released.

sections.name: Non-repudiation | Validate Binding of Information Reviewer Identity
sections.description:
(a) Validate the binding of the information reviewer identity to the information at the transfer or release points prior to release or transfer between [Assignment: organization-defined security domains]; and

(b) Perform [Assignment: organization-defined actions] in the event of a validation error.

sections.name: Audit Record Retention
sections.description: Retain audit records for [Assignment: organization-defined time period consistent with records retention policy] to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of incidents and to meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

sections.name: Audit Record Retention | Long-term Retrieval Capability
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined measures] to ensure that long-term audit records generated by the system can be retrieved.

sections.name: Audit Record Generation
Audit Record Generation | System-wide and Time-correlated Audit Trail
sections.description: Compile audit records from [Assignment: organization-defined system components] into a system-wide (logical or physical) audit trail that is time-correlated to within [Assignment: organization-defined level of tolerance for the relationship between time stamps of individual records in the audit trail].

sections.name: Audit Record Generation | Standardized Formats
sections.description: Produce a system-wide (logical or physical) audit trail composed of audit records in a standardized format.

sections.name: Audit Record Generation | Changes by Authorized Individuals
sections.description: Provide and implement the capability for [Assignment: organization-defined individuals or roles] to change the logging to be performed on [Assignment: organization-defined system components] based on [Assignment: organization-defined selectable event criteria] within [Assignment: organization-defined time thresholds].

sections.name: Audit Record Generation | Query Parameter Audits of Personally Identifiable Information
sections.description: Provide and implement the capability for auditing the parameters of user query events for data sets containing personally identifiable information.

sections.name: Monitoring for Information Disclosure
sections.description:
a. Monitor [Assignment: organization-defined open-source information and/or information sites] [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] for evidence of unauthorized disclosure of organizational information; and

b. If an information disclosure is discovered:
  1\. Notify [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]; and 
  2\. Take the following additional actions: [Assignment: organization-defined additional actions].

sections.name: Monitoring for Information Disclosure | Use of Automated Tools
sections.description: Monitor open-source information and information sites using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Monitoring for Information Disclosure | Review of Monitored Sites
sections.description: Review the list of open-source information sites being monitored [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].

sections.name: Monitoring for Information Disclosure | Unauthorized Replication of Information
sections.description: Employ discovery techniques, processes, and tools to determine if external entities are replicating organizational information in an unauthorized manner.

sections.name: Session Audit
sections.description:
a. Provide and implement the capability for [Assignment: organization-defined users or roles] to [Selection (one or more): record; view; hear; log] the content of a user session under [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances]; and

b. Develop, integrate, and use session auditing activities in consultation with legal counsel and in accordance with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines.

sections.name: Session Audit | System Start-up
sections.description: Initiate session audits automatically at system start-up.

sections.name: Session Audit | Remote Viewing and Listening
sections.description: Provide and implement the capability for authorized users to remotely view and hear content related to an established user session in real time.

sections.name: Cross-organizational Audit Logging
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined methods] for coordinating [Assignment: organization-defined audit information] among external organizations when audit information is transmitted across organizational boundaries.

sections.description: Preserve the identity of individuals in cross-organizational audit trails.

sections.name: Cross-organizational Audit Logging | Sharing of Audit Information
sections.description: Provide cross-organizational audit information to [Assignment: organization-defined organizations] based on [Assignment: organization-defined cross-organizational sharing agreements].

sections.name: Cross-organizational Audit Logging | Disassociability
sections.description: Implement [Assignment: organization-defined measures] to disassociate individuals from audit information transmitted across organizational boundaries.

  2\. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy and the associated assessment, authorization, and monitoring controls;

b. Designate an [Assignment: organization-defined official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy and procedures; and

c. Review and update the current assessment, authorization, and monitoring:
  1\. Policy [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events]; and

sections.name: Control Assessments

sections.description: Employ independent assessors or assessment teams to conduct control assessments.

sections.name: Control Assessments | Specialized Assessments
sections.description: Include as part of control assessments, [Assignment: organization-defined frequency], [Selection: announced; unannounced], [Selection (one or more): in-depth monitoring; security instrumentation; automated security test cases; vulnerability scanning; malicious user testing; insider threat assessment; performance and load testing; data leakage or data loss assessment; [Assignment: organization-defined other forms of assessment]].

sections.name: Control Assessments | Leveraging Results from External Organizations
sections.description: Leverage the results of control assessments performed by [Assignment: organization-defined external organization] on [Assignment: organization-defined system] when the assessment meets [Assignment: organization-defined requirements].

sections.name: Information Exchange

sections.description: Verify that individuals or systems transferring data between interconnecting systems have the requisite authorizations (i.e., write permissions or privileges) prior to accepting such data.

sections.name: Information Exchange | Transitive Information Exchanges
sections.description:
(a) Identify transitive (downstream) information exchanges with other systems through the systems identified in CA-3a; and

(b) Take measures to ensure that transitive (downstream) information exchanges cease when the controls on identified transitive (downstream) systems cannot be verified or validated.

sections.name: Plan of Action and Milestones
sections.description:
a. Develop a plan of action and milestones for the system to document the planned remediation actions of the organization to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during the assessment of the controls and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities in the system; and

b. Update existing plan of action and milestones [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] based on the findings from control assessments, independent audits or reviews, and continuous monitoring activities.

sections.name: Plan of Action and Milestones | Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
sections.description: Ensure the accuracy, currency, and availability of the plan of action and milestones for the system using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Authorization

sections.description: Employ a joint authorization process for the system that includes multiple authorizing officials from the same organization conducting the authorization.

sections.name: Authorization | Joint Authorization — Inter-organization
sections.description: Employ a joint authorization process for the system that includes multiple authorizing officials with at least one authorizing official from an organization external to the organization conducting the authorization.

sections.name: Continuous Monitoring
Continuous Monitoring | Independent Assessment
sections.description: Employ independent assessors or assessment teams to monitor the controls in the system on an ongoing basis.

sections.name: Continuous Monitoring | Trend Analyses
sections.description: Employ trend analyses to determine if control implementations, the frequency of continuous monitoring activities, and the types of activities used in the continuous monitoring process need to be modified based on empirical data.

sections.name: Continuous Monitoring | Risk Monitoring
sections.description:
Ensure risk monitoring is an integral part of the continuous monitoring strategy that includes the following:

(a) Effectiveness monitoring;

(b) Compliance monitoring; and

(c) Change monitoring.

sections.name: Continuous Monitoring | Consistency Analysis
sections.description: Employ the following actions to validate that policies are established and implemented controls are operating in a consistent manner: [Assignment: organization-defined actions].

sections.name: Continuous Monitoring | Automation Support for Monitoring
sections.description: Ensure the accuracy, currency, and availability of monitoring results for the system using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Penetration Testing
sections.description: Conduct penetration testing [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] on [Assignment: organization-defined systems or system components].

sections.name: Penetration Testing | Independent Penetration Testing Agent or Team
sections.description: Employ an independent penetration testing agent or team to perform penetration testing on the system or system components.

sections.name: Penetration Testing | Red Team Exercises
sections.description: Employ the following red-team exercises to simulate attempts by adversaries to compromise organizational systems in accordance with applicable rules of engagement: [Assignment: organization-defined red team exercises].

sections.name: Penetration Testing | Facility Penetration Testing
sections.description: Employ a penetration testing process that includes [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] [Selection: announced; unannounced] attempts to bypass or circumvent controls associated with physical access points to the facility.

sections.name: Internal System Connections

sections.description: Perform security and privacy compliance checks on constituent system components prior to the establishment of the internal connection.

a. Develop, document, and maintain under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the system; and

b. Review and update the baseline configuration of the system:

  2\. When required due to [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances]; and
  3\. When system components are installed or upgraded.

sections.description: Maintain the currency, completeness, accuracy, and availability of the baseline configuration of the system using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Baseline Configuration | Retention of Previous Configurations
sections.description: Retain [Assignment: organization-defined number] of previous versions of baseline configurations of the system to support rollback.

sections.name: Baseline Configuration | Development and Test Environments
sections.description: Maintain a baseline configuration for system development and test environments that is managed separately from the operational baseline configuration.

sections.name: Baseline Configuration | Configure Systems and Components for High-risk Areas
sections.description:
(a) Issue [Assignment: organization-defined systems or system components] with [Assignment: organization-defined configurations] to individuals traveling to locations that the organization deems to be of significant risk; and

(b) Apply the following controls to the systems or components when the individuals return from travel: [Assignment: organization-defined controls].

sections.name: Configuration Change Control
Configuration Change Control | Automated Documentation, Notification, and Prohibition of Changes
Configuration Change Control | Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes
sections.description: Test, validate, and document changes to the system before finalizing the implementation of the changes.

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Automated Change Implementation
sections.description: Implement changes to the current system baseline and deploy the updated baseline across the installed base using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Security and Privacy Representatives
sections.description: Require [Assignment: organization-defined security and privacy representatives] to be members of the [Assignment: organization-defined configuration change control element].

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Automated Security Response
sections.description: Implement the following security responses automatically if baseline configurations are changed in an unauthorized manner: [Assignment: organization-defined security responses].

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Cryptography Management
sections.description: Ensure that cryptographic mechanisms used to provide the following controls are under configuration management: [Assignment: organization-defined controls].

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Review System Changes
sections.description: Review changes to the system [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] or when [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances] to determine whether unauthorized changes have occurred.

sections.name: Configuration Change Control | Prevent or Restrict Configuration Changes
sections.description: Prevent or restrict changes to the configuration of the system under the following circumstances: [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances].

sections.name: Impact Analyses
sections.description: Analyze changes to the system to determine potential security and privacy impacts prior to change implementation.

sections.name: Impact Analyses | Separate Test Environments
sections.description: Analyze changes to the system in a separate test environment before implementation in an operational environment, looking for security and privacy impacts due to flaws, weaknesses, incompatibility, or intentional malice.

sections.name: Impact Analyses | Verification of Controls
sections.description: After system changes, verify that the impacted controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome with regard to meeting the security and privacy requirements for the system.

sections.name: Access Restrictions for Change
sections.description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system.

sections.name: Access Restrictions for Change | Automated Access Enforcement and Audit Records
sections.description:
(a) Enforce access restrictions using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms]; and 

(b) Automatically generate audit records of the enforcement actions.

sections.name: Access Restrictions for Change | Dual Authorization
sections.description: Enforce dual authorization for implementing changes to [Assignment: organization-defined system components and system-level information].

sections.name: Access Restrictions for Change | Privilege Limitation for Production and Operation
sections.description:
(a) Limit privileges to change system components and system-related information within a production or operational environment; and

(b) Review and reevaluate privileges [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].

sections.name: Access Restrictions for Change | Limit Library Privileges
sections.description: Limit privileges to change software resident within software libraries.

sections.name: Configuration Settings

sections.description: Manage, apply, and verify configuration settings for [Assignment: organization-defined system components] using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Configuration Settings | Respond to Unauthorized Changes
sections.description: Take the following actions in response to unauthorized changes to [Assignment: organization-defined configuration settings]: [Assignment: organization-defined actions].

sections.name: Least Functionality | Periodic Review
sections.description:
(a) Review the system [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] to identify unnecessary and/or nonsecure functions, ports, protocols, software, and services; and

(b) Disable or remove [Assignment: organization-defined functions, ports, protocols, software, and services within the system deemed to be unnecessary and/or nonsecure].

sections.name: Least Functionality | Prevent Program Execution
sections.description: Prevent program execution in accordance with [Selection (one or more): [Assignment: organization-defined policies, rules of behavior, and/or access agreements regarding software program usage and restrictions]; rules authorizing the terms and conditions of software program usage].

sections.name: Least Functionality | Registration Compliance
sections.description: Ensure compliance with [Assignment: organization-defined registration requirements for functions, ports, protocols, and services].

sections.name: Least Functionality | Unauthorized Software — Deny-by-exception
Least Functionality | Authorized Software — Allow-by-exception
Least Functionality | Confined Environments with Limited Privileges
sections.description: Require that the following user-installed software execute in a confined physical or virtual machine environment with limited privileges: [Assignment: organization-defined user-installed software].

sections.name: Least Functionality | Code Execution in Protected Environments
sections.description:
Allow execution of binary or machine-executable code only in confined physical or virtual machine environments and with the explicit approval of [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] when such code is:

(a) Obtained from sources with limited or no warranty; and/or

(b) Without the provision of source code.

sections.name: Least Functionality | Binary or Machine Executable Code
sections.description:
(a) Prohibit the use of binary or machine-executable code from sources with limited or no warranty or without the provision of source code; and

(b) Allow exceptions only for compelling mission or operational requirements and with the approval of the authorizing official.

sections.description: Enforce and monitor compliance with software installation policies using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.description: Use automated tools to identify [Assignment: organization-defined information by information type] on [Assignment: organization-defined system components] to ensure controls are in place to protect organizational information and individual privacy.

sections.description: Plan for the resumption of [Selection: all; essential] mission and business functions within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] of contingency plan activation.

sections.description: Plan for the transfer of [Selection: all; essential] mission and business functions to alternate processing and/or storage sites with minimal or no loss of operational continuity and sustain that continuity through system restoration to primary processing and/or storage sites.

sections.name: Contingency Plan | Coordinate with External Service Providers
sections.description: Coordinate the contingency plan with the contingency plans of external service providers to ensure that contingency requirements can be satisfied.

sections.name: Contingency Plan | Identify Critical Assets
sections.description: Identify critical system assets supporting [Selection: all; essential] mission and business functions.

sections.name: Contingency Training
sections.description:
a. Provide contingency training to system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities: 
  1\. Within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] of assuming a contingency role or responsibility;
  2\. When required by system changes; and
  3\. [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] thereafter; and

b. Review and update contingency training content [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events].

sections.name: Contingency Training | Simulated Events
sections.description: Incorporate simulated events into contingency training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations.

sections.name: Contingency Training | Mechanisms Used in Training Environments
sections.description: Employ mechanisms used in operations to provide a more thorough and realistic contingency training environment.

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing
sections.description:
a. Test the contingency plan for the system [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] using  the following tests to determine the effectiveness of the plan and the readiness to execute the plan: [Assignment: organization-defined tests].

b. Review the contingency plan test results; and

c. Initiate corrective actions, if needed.

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing | Coordinate with Related Plans
sections.description: Coordinate contingency plan testing with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing | Alternate Processing Site
sections.description:
Test the contingency plan at the alternate processing site:

(a) To familiarize contingency personnel with the facility and available resources; and

(b) To evaluate the capabilities of the alternate processing site to support contingency operations.

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing | Automated Testing
sections.description: Test the contingency plan using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing | Full Recovery and Reconstitution
sections.description: Include a full recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state as part of contingency plan testing.

sections.name: Contingency Plan Testing | Self-challenge
sections.description: Employ [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms] to [Assignment: organization-defined system or system component] to disrupt and adversely affect the system or system component.

sections.name: Alternate Storage Site
sections.description:
a. Establish an alternate storage site, including necessary agreements to permit the storage and retrieval of system backup information; and

b. Ensure that the alternate storage site provides controls equivalent to that of the primary site.

sections.name: Alternate Storage Site | Separation from Primary Site
sections.description: Identify an alternate storage site that is sufficiently separated from the primary storage site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

sections.name: Alternate Storage Site | Recovery Time and Recovery Point Objectives
sections.description: Configure the alternate storage site to facilitate recovery operations in accordance with recovery time and recovery point objectives.

sections.name: Alternate Storage Site | Accessibility
sections.description: Identify potential accessibility problems to the alternate storage site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster and outline explicit mitigation actions.

sections.name: Alternate Processing Site
Alternate Processing Site | Separation from Primary Site
sections.description: Identify an alternate processing site that is sufficiently separated from the primary processing site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

sections.name: Alternate Processing Site | Accessibility
sections.description: Identify potential accessibility problems to alternate processing sites in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster and outlines explicit mitigation actions.

sections.name: Alternate Processing Site | Priority of Service
sections.description: Develop alternate processing site agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with availability requirements (including recovery time objectives).

sections.name: Alternate Processing Site | Preparation for Use
sections.description: Prepare the alternate processing site so that the site can serve as the operational site supporting essential mission and business functions.

sections.name: Alternate Processing Site | Inability to Return to Primary Site
sections.description: Plan and prepare for circumstances that preclude returning to the primary processing site.

sections.name: Telecommunications Services
sections.description: Establish alternate telecommunications services, including necessary agreements to permit the resumption of [Assignment: organization-defined system operations] for essential mission and business functions within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when the primary telecommunications capabilities are unavailable at either the primary or alternate processing or storage sites.

sections.name: Telecommunications Services | Priority of Service Provisions
sections.description:
(a) Develop primary and alternate telecommunications service agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with availability requirements (including recovery time objectives); and

(b) Request Telecommunications Service Priority for all telecommunications services used for national security emergency preparedness if the primary and/or alternate telecommunications services are provided by a common carrier.

sections.name: Telecommunications Services | Single Points of Failure
sections.description: Obtain alternate telecommunications services to reduce the likelihood of sharing a single point of failure with primary telecommunications services.

sections.name: Telecommunications Services | Separation of Primary and Alternate Providers
sections.description: Obtain alternate telecommunications services from providers that are separated from primary service providers to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

sections.name: Telecommunications Services | Provider Contingency Plan
Telecommunications Services | Alternate Telecommunication Service Testing
sections.description: Test alternate telecommunication services [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].

sections.name: System Backup
System Backup | Testing for Reliability and Integrity
sections.description: Test backup information [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] to verify media reliability and information integrity.

sections.name: System Backup | Test Restoration Using Sampling
sections.description: Use a sample of backup information in the restoration of selected system functions as part of contingency plan testing.

sections.name: System Backup | Separate Storage for Critical Information
sections.description: Store backup copies of [Assignment: organization-defined critical system software and other security-related information] in a separate facility or in a fire rated container that is not collocated with the operational system.

sections.name: System Backup | Transfer to Alternate Storage Site
sections.description: Transfer system backup information to the alternate storage site [Assignment: organization-defined time period and transfer rate consistent with the recovery time and recovery point objectives].

sections.name: System Backup | Redundant Secondary System
sections.description: Conduct system backup by maintaining a redundant secondary system that is not collocated with the primary system and that can be activated without loss of information or disruption to operations.

sections.name: System Backup | Dual Authorization for Deletion or Destruction
sections.description: Enforce dual authorization for the deletion or destruction of [Assignment: organization-defined backup information].

sections.name: System Backup | Cryptographic Protection
